segments.beat: Hand holds device face-on toward camera; lit display showing pixel game content visible through transparent pane; clear shell reveals PCB/internals underneath
Device rotated and tilted; golden PCB board fully visible through clear resin shell from side/rear angle; display still partially lit
Continued rotation showing underside and rear of device; transparent shell from multiple angles exposes full board layout; D-pad and A/B buttons visible on face
Camera pulls slightly back; device held in palm showing pocket scale; shell internals and display face both in frame
Close macro shot of display face; see-through display lit with pixel/icon content (snowflake-style graphics); transparent pane clearly shows game pixels floating on clear background
Device angled to show profile; golden board and component traces visible through clear shell at oblique angle; display active
Return to overhead/table angle; device placed in hand against neutral background; full form factor visible, controls and transparent body clear

demonstrates.motion_value: Rotation through multiple angles lets viewer track the golden PCB and component traces through the shell continuously — a still only shows one angle; motion proves the transparency is full 360 not a windowed cutout

Macro approach and slight angle shift reveals pixel content (snowflake/icon graphics) visibly floating on a transparent pane with background visible behind them — motion confirms it is genuinely see-through, not a reflective effect
Display shows active pixel game content from the first frame; D-pad and A/B layout glimpsed as hand repositions — motion confirms the controls are physical and the screen is live
Brief pull-back shows device sitting in a single palm; scale is established relative to hand size — motion transitions from close to context shot making the size read clearly
Slow deliberate rotation mimics the way someone turns over an object they find beautiful — the handling gesture itself signals it is an object worth inspecting, not just using
As shell catches light during rotation, resin clarity and edge sharpness are visible; motion catches specular highlights that confirm optical-grade finish

records.selection_note: 13s hand-rotation demo nails both load-bearing claims (internals through shell + lit transparent display) in a single continuous motion; trims cleanly to a 6-8s hero loop on the macro display beat.

aesthetics.standout: display legible in close-up frames around 0:06-0:08
aesthetics.weakness: handheld prototype demo on bare desk - no controlled framing or cinematic intent
aesthetics.note: raw maker footage; hand holding device over a wood-grain desk with flat overhead light - needs a clean-surface tripod reshoot with intentional lighting before paid placement

segments.beat: Hand holds device close to camera against window/plant background; transparent shell clearly visible with PCB internals showing through, screen off or dim
Device rotated slightly, screen lights up with pixel game content on transparent display pane; PCB and components visible through clear enclosure
Camera pulls back slightly revealing device at natural hand-held scale against indoor window light; full device form factor visible — D-pad and A/B buttons visible on front
Slow pan/tilt around device while lit; plant and window visible in background establishing casual lifestyle context
Device held at slight angle emphasizing transparent shell depth; internal board components clearly silhouetted through resin body
Screen active with white pixel game graphics on transparent pane; hand grip shows pocket-scale size, device face-on to camera
Close macro-style shot of device face; pixel game content bright on transparent display, PCB internals visible simultaneously through shell — dual transparency payoff
Final hold on device face; clip ends

demonstrates.motion_value: Continuous slow rotation and angle shifts let viewer confirm transparency is a physical property of the shell in 3D, not a lens trick or graphic — a still from any single angle is weaker proof
Screen illuminates while PCB remains simultaneously visible through it; motion allows viewer to track that both layers coexist at multiple orientations, proving the display is genuinely transparent
Hand grip throughout video naturalistically shows true size relative to human palm; motion contextualizes it as a held object rather than a staged flat lay
Pixel game graphics visible on screen and D-pad/button layout visible on device face; no active button-pressing shown so partial rather than strong
Slow deliberate rotation and lifestyle window-light staging conveys desirable object framing; no dedicated display/shelf shot so only partial
Angled shots in natural window light catch resin surface finish and clean edges; motion highlights depth and build clarity better than a flat still

records.selection_note: 14s close-up hand-held loop with continuous transparency payoff — both PCB-through-shell and lit see-through display proven simultaneously across multiple angles; strongest single asset for lead hero use.

aesthetics.standout: natural window light gives a livable context
aesthetics.weakness: handheld close-up with no lock-off; device fills too little frame, constant micro-drift, screen detail unreadable
aesthetics.note: hobbyist demo footage; needs tripod, deliberate framing, and a hero angle that shows the screen legibly

segments.beat: Close overhead shot of device held in hand, screen facing up — display shows a checkerboard/pixel pattern, clear shell and PCB visible through enclosure; hand rotates device slightly
Continued close hold, screen brightly lit with checkerboard pattern, transparent shell clearly shows internal PCB components; device tilted to show side profile
Camera pulls back slightly, device held in palm showing full form factor — side and rear faces reveal PCB through clear resin; device oriented horizontally showing landscape profile
Device rotated to show front face head-on with screen displaying vertical bar/column graphic; clear shell and board internals visible; slow lateral rotation continues
Device held in fingertips, rotated to near-side profile — transparent side walls show PCB traces and components; screen displays narrow column graphic
Camera angle shifts slightly, device still in hand, screen now showing bright checkerboard again; clearer view of transparent top face
Device lifted and tilted — bright lit checkerboard display; transparent enclosure walls show PCB clearly from front-angle; hand moves device away from camera
Background shifts to reveal green plant/foliage backdrop; device held at arms length showing pocket-scale in context of natural environment; checkerboard display lit
Device brought closer to camera against plant background — screen brightly lit with checkerboard, transparent body shows PCB internals; good scale reference against hand
Background transitions to neutral/architectural setting; device held in two-hand grip showing full front face — checkerboard display, clear shell and board visible; device tilted and rotated
Device rotated showing top/back faces — PCB and internal components clearly visible through transparent shell panels; checkerboard display toggling on/off during rotation
Device tilted showing rear and side faces against light architectural background — internal PCB fully exposed through clear resin walls; display shows bright checkerboard; continued slow rotation
Final frames: device held steady showing front face with lit checkerboard display — bright, clean closing shot against neutral background

demonstrates.motion_value: Continuous slow rotation cycles through all four shell faces, showing PCB and board components through each transparent wall in turn — a still captures one angle but motion proves the full-enclosure transparency

Display stays lit with bright checkerboard pattern throughout; rotation shifts the viewing angle so the pixel-on-transparent-pane effect is visible from multiple perspectives, confirming it is the display surface and not a reflective artifact
Device is held in a single hand throughout with fingers wrapping around it — continuous hand presence across all angles gives persistent scale reference that a single still would not sustain
Slow deliberate rotation in hand mimics the way a collectible is turned over for inspection; no shelf/display context but the handling gesture implies object admiration
Pull-back and lateral rotation reveals clean edges and uniform resin finish across all shell faces; motion shows surface consistency around the full perimeter rather than a single polished angle
Display shows a checkerboard pixel pattern which suggests a game or test screen; D-pad/A/B controls are not clearly visible in any frame — claim is inferred from display content only

records.selection_note: Sustained 360-style hand-rotation delivers the two load-bearing transparency claims simultaneously across the full clip; no controls or hackable features are shown, so retro_gaming and hackable_maker evidence is weak.

aesthetics.standout: display lit and visible at multiple angles
aesthetics.weakness: handheld prototype footage - soft focus, cluttered/busy backgrounds (plants, desk debris)

slow deliberate hold at face level mimics handling a display object; ambient light interaction confirms aesthetic intent
checkerboard pixel pattern on display is consistent with retro game content but no controller interaction shown

low — no hand or reference object; scale inferrable only from known dimensions

records.selection_note: Slow cinematic orbit over colorful surface with lit see-through display running end-to-end — strongest single clip for leading with the transparency + display-on hero claim simultaneously.

aesthetics.standout: bold cyan UI text pops against neutral device body
aesthetics.weakness: loud harlequin-patchwork background dominates and cheapens the device
aesthetics.note: continuous slow orbit/zoom on a busy colored-block surface - background + flat lighting kill ad viability
